sentences.question: How do you casually greet someone?
sentences.phonics: Hi there! (친근한 인사) → How's it (어떻게) → going? (진행되고 있니)
sentences.tips: Hi / Hello / Hey | 상황별 인사 강도 차이

sentences.question: How do you casually greet someone?
sentences.phonics: Hey! (호출) → What's up, (무슨 일) → dude? (친구)
sentences.tips: Hi / Hello / Hey | 상황별 인사 강도 차이

sentences.question: What do you say when you meet someone again?
sentences.phonics: Good to see you (만나서 좋아) → again! (다시)
sentences.tips: 감탄문/명령문 패턴 | 감정 표현 또는 강한 지시

sentences.question: What do you say when meeting someone you haven't seen in a while?
sentences.phonics: Long time (오랜 시간) → no see! (못 봤네)
sentences.tips: 감탄문/명령문 패턴 | 감정 표현 또는 강한 지시

sentences.question: How do you ask about a way or condition?
sentences.phonics: How's it (어떻게) → going? (되어가고 있니)
sentences.tips: 의문문 패턴 | 질문으로 상대방 의도/정보 확인

sentences.question: How do you ask about something specific?
sentences.phonics: What's new (새로운 게 뭐야) → with you? (너에게)
sentences.tips: 의문문 패턴 | 질문으로 상대방 의도/정보 확인

sentences.english: How was your day at school?
sentences.question: How do you ask how something went?
sentences.phonics: How was (어땠어) → your day (너의 하루) → at school? (학교에서)
sentences.tips: 의문문 패턴 | 질문으로 상대방 의도/정보 확인

sentences.question: What do you say when saying goodbye?
sentences.phonics: See you (보자) → tomorrow (내일) → at school! (학교에서)
sentences.tips: 감탄문/명령문 패턴 | 감정 표현 또는 강한 지시

sentences.question: What do you say when saying goodbye?
sentences.phonics: Take care! (조심해) → See you (보자) → later! (나중에)
sentences.tips: 감탄문/명령문 패턴 | 감정 표현 또는 강한 지시

sentences.question: What do you say when saying goodbye?
sentences.phonics: Have (가지다) → a good one! (좋은 것) → Bye! (작별)
sentences.tips: Have + 과거분사? | 경험 질문 / Have a ~ | 표현

sentences.question: How do you introduce yourself?
sentences.phonics: I'm from (나는 출신) → [도시], (도시) → [나라]. (나라)
sentences.tips: 기본 문장 패턴 | 핵심 실용 표현

sentences.question: How do you introduce yourself?
sentences.phonics: I'm in (나는 있어) → the [숫자]th (몇 번째) → grade. (학년)
sentences.tips: 기본 문장 패턴 | 핵심 실용 표현

sentences.question: How do you talk about yourself or your interests?
sentences.phonics: My favorite (내가 제일 좋아하는) → subject (과목) → is [과목]. (이다)
sentences.tips: 소유격 + 명사 | 소유 관계 표현

sentences.english: I have a dog named Max.
sentences.question: How do you say what you have?
sentences.phonics: I have (나는 있어) → a [동물] (동물) → named [이름]. (이름이 ~인)
sentences.tips: I + want/need/have + 명사 | 기본 욕구/소유 표현

sentences.question: How do you talk about something you are into?
sentences.phonics: I'm really (나는 정말) → into (빠져) → [취미]. (무엇에)
sentences.tips: 기본 문장 패턴 | 핵심 실용 표현

sentences.question: How do you talk about your skills or interests?
sentences.phonics: I'm good (나는 잘해) → at (에서) → [동명사]. (무엇을)
sentences.tips: 기본 문장 패턴 | 핵심 실용 표현

sentences.english: My dream is to be a doctor.
sentences.question: How do you talk about yourself or your interests?
sentences.phonics: My dream (내 꿈) → is to be (되는 것) → [직업]. (직업)
sentences.tips: 소유격 + 명사 | 소유 관계 표현

sentences.question: How do you say what you have?
sentences.phonics: I have (있어) → [숫자] (개수) → [가족]. (누가)
sentences.tips: I + want/need/have + 명사 | 기본 욕구/소유 표현

sentences.question: How do you say where you live?
sentences.phonics: I live (나는 살아) → in [장소]. (어디에)
sentences.tips: 기본 문장 패턴 | 핵심 실용 표현

sentences.question: What do you say when ending a conversation?
sentences.phonics: Nice (좋았어) → talking (이야기하는 게) → to you! (너와)
sentences.tips: 감탄문/명령문 패턴 | 감정 표현 또는 강한 지시

sentences.english: I'm so excited about the trip!
sentences.question: How do you express your feelings in English?
sentences.phonics: I'm so excited (너무 기대돼) → about (에 대해) → [명사]. (무엇이)
sentences.tips: 감탄문/명령문 패턴 | 감정 표현 또는 강한 지시

sentences.question: How do you express your feelings in English?
sentences.phonics: I'm really (나는 정말) → nervous. (긴장된)
sentences.tips: 짧은 핵심 표현 | 자주 쓰는 일상 문구

sentences.question: How do you express that something made you very happy?
sentences.answer: 그게 오늘 완전 기분 좋게 해줬어!
sentences.phonics: That really (그게 정말) → made (만들어줬어) → my day! (내 하루를)
sentences.tips: 감탄문/명령문 패턴 | 감정 표현 또는 강한 지시

sentences.english: I'm in a really good mood today!
sentences.question: How do you describe your current situation?
sentences.phonics: I'm in (나는 있어) → a good mood (좋은 기분에) → today! (오늘)
sentences.tips: 감탄문/명령문 패턴 | 감정 표현 또는 강한 지시

sentences.english: I'm feeling a bit under the weather.
sentences.question: How do you say how you're feeling?
sentences.phonics: I'm feeling (나는 느껴) → a bit (조금) → under the weather. (몸 안 좋은)
sentences.tips: 기본 문장 패턴 | 핵심 실용 표현

sentences.english: I wake up at 7 every morning.
sentences.question: How do you talk about your daily sleep schedule?
sentences.phonics: I wake up (일어나) → at [시간] (몇 시에) → every morning. (매일 아침)
sentences.tips: 기본 문장 패턴 | 핵심 실용 표현

sentences.english: I always have breakfast before school.
sentences.question: How do you talk about your daily habits or routine?
sentences.answer: 나는 항상 학교 가기 전에 아침을 먹어.
sentences.phonics: I always have (항상 먹어) → breakfast (아침) → before school. (학교 가기 전에)
sentences.tips: 기본 문장 패턴 | 핵심 실용 표현

sentences.english: I study for two hours after school.
sentences.question: How do you talk about your daily activities?
sentences.answer: 나는 방과 후에 두 시간 공부해.
sentences.phonics: I study (공부해) → for [시간] (얼마나) → after school. (방과 후에)
sentences.tips: 기본 문장 패턴 | 핵심 실용 표현

sentences.question: How do you talk about activities or classes you do regularly?
sentences.phonics: I go to bed (나는 자) → at [시간]. (몇 시에)
sentences.tips: 기본 문장 패턴 | 핵심 실용 표현

sentences.question: How do you talk about your daily activities?
sentences.phonics: I practice (연습해) → [악기/기술] (무엇을) → every day. (매일)
sentences.tips: 기본 문장 패턴 | 핵심 실용 표현

sentences.question: How do you make a polite request?
sentences.phonics: Can I borrow (빌릴 수 있어?) → your [물건]? (네 것을)
sentences.tips: 의문문 패턴 | 질문으로 상대방 의도/정보 확인

sentences.question: How do you say you don't have or can't do something?
sentences.phonics: I don't understand (이해 못 해) → this problem. (이 문제를)
sentences.tips: 부정문 표현 | don't/can't + 동사

sentences.question: How do you describe something?
sentences.phonics: The test (시험이) → was really (정말 ~했어) → tough. (어려운)
sentences.tips: 기본 문장 패턴 | 핵심 실용 표현

sentences.english: I forgot to do my homework!
sentences.question: What do you say at school?
sentences.phonics: I forgot (깜빡했어) → to do (하는 것을) → my homework! (숙제를)
sentences.tips: 감탄문/명령문 패턴 | 감정 표현 또는 강한 지시

sentences.question: How do you suggest doing something together?
sentences.phonics: Let's work on (같이 해 보자) → this (이것을) → together. (함께)
sentences.tips: Let's + 동사원형 | 함께 하자 제안

sentences.english: What do you do for fun?
sentences.question: How do you ask someone about their opinion or action?
sentences.phonics: What do you (뭐 해) → do (하니) → for fun? (재미로)
sentences.tips: What + 동사? | 정보 요청 의문문

sentences.english: I'm on the school soccer team.
sentences.question: How do you say you're on a team or in a group?
sentences.phonics: I'm on (나는 있어) → the school (학교의) → [스포츠] team. (팀에)
sentences.tips: 기본 문장 패턴 | 핵심 실용 표현

sentences.english: Want to hang out this weekend?
sentences.question: How do you casually invite someone to do something?
sentences.phonics: Want to (하고 싶어?) → hang out (어울려 놀다) → this weekend? (이번 주말에)
sentences.tips: 의문문 패턴 | 질문으로 상대방 의도/정보 확인

sentences.question: How do you say something was really fun or enjoyable?
sentences.phonics: That was (그게 ~했어) → so much (너무 많이) → fun! (재미있었어)
sentences.tips: 감탄문/명령문 패턴 | 감정 표현 또는 강한 지시

sentences.question: How do you say something about someone?
sentences.phonics: You're (너는) → my best (내 가장 친한) → friend! (친구야)
sentences.tips: 감탄문/명령문 패턴 | 감정 표현 또는 강한 지시
